139797.explanation: DENK Tilburg supports culture, but the subsidy to Zuidelijk Toneel is too high and must be distributed more fairly.

139798.explanation: Culture is important for social interaction, development, and accessibility.

139799.explanation: The artwork is expensive and lacks broad support. That money could be better spent on neighborhood facilities.

139800.explanation: It is unlikely that young people will comply with this. Enforcement and dispersing crowds are more effective.

139801.explanation: More visible enforcement increases safety, especially in vulnerable neighborhoods like Tilburg North and Tilburg West.

139802.explanation: Coffeeshops normalize drug use and cause nuisance, particularly in vulnerable neighborhoods.

139803.explanation: Extra supervision helps limit nuisance and drug-related crime.

139804.explanation: This encourages drug use and additional traffic and is irresponsible.

139805.explanation: Freedom of religion and equal employment opportunities are paramount.

139807.explanation: The Netherlands has too much livestock farming. Municipal land should be used for more sustainable and future-proof functions.

139808.explanation: Local ties are important. Residents of Tilburg should not consistently be left behind.

139809.explanation: This is necessary to address the housing shortage fairly.

139810.explanation: Vacancy is unacceptable during a housing shortage.

139811.explanation: Building is necessary, but so is greenery. Trees may be cut down provided that at least two new trees are planted elsewhere for each tree removed.

139812.explanation: That is insufficient to solve the housing shortage.

139813.explanation: Customization is needed. Many elderly people often move in their own interest. Targeted support is better than a standard premium.

139814.explanation: Needed for starters and singles, provided it is affordable and of good quality.

139815.explanation: Benefits are a social safety net, not a punishment. Mandatory counter-performances increase pressure and displace regular work. Voluntary participation with guidance works better.

139816.explanation: Animal care must not be a luxury. Municipal support prevents animal suffering and financial problems for low-income people.

139818.explanation: Youth work prevents problems and strengthens opportunities. Budget cuts are short-sighted and lead to higher social costs.

139819.explanation: Two-way traffic increases traffic pressure and unsafe situations. The current layout contributes to visibility, traffic safety, and livability in the center.

139820.explanation: Parking spaces are essential for residents, caregivers, and entrepreneurs. Reducing them increases parking pressure and mainly affects families and the elderly.

139821.explanation: Ringbaan-West is an important traffic artery. Four lanes ensure flow and prevent shortcut traffic in residential areas.

139822.explanation: The environmental zone mainly affects people with low incomes. Sustainability must be achievable and socially just, not punitive.

139823.explanation: Paid parking raises costs for residents and visitors. Parking in residential neighborhoods must remain affordable and accessible.

139824.explanation: More speed bumps increase safety, especially for children and the elderly. Livability and safety take precedence over speed.

139825.explanation: A night bus increases safety and accessibility, especially for people without a car or with irregular working hours.

139826.explanation: Equal rights also mean space for religious cemeteries. This fits an inclusive city.

139827.explanation: Care aligns better when people feel understood. Freedom of choice increases trust and quality of care.

139828.explanation: Bus stops are public places. Protecting the health of travelers and staff is paramount.

139829.explanation: You don’t solve nuisance by putting people out of sight. Investing in guidance, care, and prevention is more effective.

139830.explanation: Living together prevents loneliness and helps elderly people remain independent longer.

statements.title: During Carnival there should be a separate square for young people aged 17 and under.
The Ringbaan-West should remain a four-lane road.
The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.moreInfo: Property tax (ozb) is a municipal tax. This tax covers buildings such as homes and shops. Owners of homes pay this tax. For commercial properties the owners and sometimes the users pay. The amount of ozb is not the same in every municipality. The municipal council decides how much ozb people must pay. The council also decides what the municipality spends the ozb revenue on.

statements.title: The municipality should allow new homes in Tilburg that are smaller than 50 square meters.
The neighborhood and village councils should receive more funding.
statements.moreInfo: In Tilburg there are 22 neighborhood and village councils. How much money they can spend depends on subsidies they receive from the municipality. Residents can propose ideas and the municipal council decides whether to grant subsidies for them. The neighborhood and village councils participate in those decisions. They therefore do not receive a fixed annual amount of money from the municipality.

statements.title: For every tree cut down, the municipality should plant two new trees.
statements.moreInfo: According to the municipality of Tilburg, there are about 105,000 trees (not including forests). Sometimes a tree must be removed, for example if it is diseased. At the moment the municipality plants one new tree in return, either in the same spot or nearby.

statements.title: On business parks at the edge of the city there should be more large housing complexes for labor migrants.

titleParts.glossary: People from another country who work temporarily in the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should stop maintaining the Draaiend Huis, an artwork on the Hasselt roundabout.
statements.moreInfo: The artwork Draaiend Huis (Turning House) was created by John Körmeling. It stands on the Hasselt roundabout. The work is a freestanding terraced house that rotates around the roundabout in the direction of traffic. Since the end of 2024 the artwork has been stationary because its technical installation needs to be replaced.

People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

There should be more camera surveillance in Tilburg's nightlife district.
There should be a free public toilet on every square in the city center.

The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

statements.moreInfo: Youth work is assistance and guidance for young people, on the street and in several youth centers in neighborhoods. In Tilburg this is provided by welfare organizations.
